title: Riser mounted controllable orifice choke
abstract: An apparatus includes a variable orifice choke disposed within a riser. The riser is connected between a drilling platform and a wellbore. A control unit is in signal communication with the variable orifice choke. The control unit is operable to control a flow area of the variable orifice choke such that a selected fluid pressure is maintained in the wellbore.
claims:
Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus, comprising: a variable orifice choke disposed within a riser, the riser connected between a drilling platform and a wellbore; and a control unit in signal communication with the variable orifice choke, the control unit operable to control a flow area of the variable orifice choke such that a selected fluid pressure is maintained in the wellbore. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 wherein the variable orifice choke comprises a housing having a coupling at longitudinal ends thereof and a closure element operable to adjust a cross sectional flow area through an interior of the housing. 3. The apparatus of claim 2 wherein the closure element comprises an iris type variable flow orifice. 4. The apparatus of claim 2 wherein the closure element comprises an inflatable bladder. 5. The apparatus of claim 2 wherein the coupling at each longitudinal end comprises a mating flange. 6. The apparatus of claim 2 wherein an internal diameter of the closure element is substantially the same as in internal diameter of the riser when the closure element is fully opened. 7. The apparatus of claim 1 further comprising an umbilical line forming signal connection between the variable orifice choke and the control unit. 8. The apparatus of claim 1 wherein a lower end of the riser comprises a lower marine riser package coupled to a well pressure control apparatus, the well pressure control apparatus coupled to an upper end of a casing disposed in the wellbore. 9. A method, comprising: 

 automatically controlling a cross sectional flow area in a segment of a riser connected between a wellbore and a drilling platform such that flow of fluid returning to the drilling platform from the wellbore is restricted so as to maintain a selected fluid pressure in the wellbore. 10. The method of claim 9 wherein the cross sectional flow area is controlled by a variable orifice choke positioned in the riser. 11. The method of claim 10 wherein the variable orifice choke comprises a remotely operable closure element. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein the remotely operable closure element comprises an inflatable bladder. 13. The method of claim 11 wherein the remotely operable closure element comprises an iris type variable flow orifice. 14. The method of claim 10 wherein an internal diameter of the variable orifice choke is substantially the same as an internal diameter of the riser when the variable orifice choke is fully opened.
